I'll compute a combined on-target score as the sum of these two, and the specificity ratio as: max(E12, P Promoter) / max(all other class scores).

Hmm — but the instructions say to rank by specificity ratio first, then break ties by absolute on-target score. #1 has the highest ratio (1.2696) so it ranks first, even though #2 has a higher absolute P Promoter score. Let me also consider #6 which has ratio 1.2088 but by far the highest absolute score (10.700). It falls to rank 6.

Actually, re-reading: I should use both E12 and P Promoter as on-target. Let me compute a combined metric. The instruction says "on-target score" — for cardiac, both E12 and P Promoter are relevant. I'll use the maximum of the two relevant classes as the on-target score, since that's what drives the specificity calculation.
